definition: AI has not produced a measurable aggregate TFP breakout. Nonfarm TFP remains at ~1.5-2.0% with no structural break. Firm-level gains are real (14% customer support, 30-40% writing tasks) but aggregate productivity = micro gains × adoption rate × reallocation efficiency × complementary investment, with each multiplicative term well below 1.0.
evidence: Aggregate nonfarm TFP data shows no structural break
Historical GPT diffusion base rates: electricity ~30 years, IT ~15 years
Term premium decomposition has not endorsed a productivity story
Firm-level gains documented but aggregate transmission incomplete
open_questions: Will AI's recursive nature compress the historical GPT diffusion timeline?
Does U.S.-China technology decoupling structurally delay AI productivity materialization?
Is the 3-5 year or 15-25 year horizon for aggregate gains more likely?
